figures.caption: FIG. 2. Top panel: comparison between the analytical series solution (black solid line) and numerical approximations for α = 0.7, a = 0.5, T = 2, y0 = 1.0, h = 0.001, and bcoeffs = [1.0, 0.2, −0.05]: Euler (red dashed), RK4 mesh-aligned (blue dotted), and RK4 with interpolation (green dash-dot). Bottom panel: relative error versus time (logarithmic scale) for the same schemes.
FIG. 6. Comparison of analytic series, Euler (L1), and L2–1σ schemes.

FIG. 3. Top panel: comparison between the analytical series solution (black solid line) and numerical approximations for α = 0.7, a = 0.5, T = 3, y0 = 1.0, h = 0.001, and bcoeffs = [1.0, 0.2, −0.05]: Euler (red dashed), RK4 mesh-aligned (blue dotted), and RK4 with interpolation (green dash-dot). Bottom panel: relative error versus time (logarithmic scale) for the same schemes.
TABLE IV. Relative errors for the numerical schemes with parameters α = 0.7, a = 0.5, T = 5, y0 = 1.0, h = 0.001, bcoeffs = [1.0, 0.2, −0.05].

FIG. 5. Top panel: comparison between the analytical series solution (black solid line) and numerical approximations for α = 0.7, a = 1.1, T = 3, y0 = 1.0, h = 0.001, and bcoeffs = [1.0, 0.2, −0.05]: Euler (red dashed), RK4 mesh-aligned (blue dotted), and RK4 with interpolation (green dash-dot). Bottom panel: relative error versus time (logarithmic scale) for the same schemes.
TABLE II. Relative errors for the numerical schemes with parameters α = 0.7, a = 0.5, T = 2, y0 = 1.0, h = 0.001, bcoeffs = [1.0, 0.2, −0.05].

FIG. 1. Top panel: comparison between the analytical series solution (black solid line) and numerical approximations for α = 0.7, a = 0.5, T = 0.7, y0 = 1.0, h = 0.001, and bcoeffs = [1.0, 0.2, −0.05]: Euler (red dashed), RK4 mesh-aligned (blue dotted), and RK4 with interpolation (green dash-dot). Bottom panel: relative error versus time (logarithmic scale) for the same schemes.

FIG. 4. Top panel: comparison between the analytical series solution (black solid line) and numerical approximations for α = 0.7, a = 0.5, T = 5, y0 = 1.0, h = 0.001, and bcoeffs = [1.0, 0.2, −0.05]: Euler (red dashed), RK4 mesh-aligned (blue dotted), and RK4 with interpolation (green dash-dot). Bottom panel: relative error versus time (logarithmic scale) for the same schemes.

FIG. 7. Comparison of analytic truncated series and series–anchored predictor–corrector algorithm for the Caputo fractional–delay problem with α = 0.7 and T = 1.0. Top panel: numerical solution y(t) from the predictor–corrector algorithm (dashed blue) versus analytic series expansion (solid black). Bottom panel: absolute error (red) and relative error (green, log scale) between the two solutions.
